etymology.description: The word 工作(工作) (gōngzuò, "work") consists of two characters. 工(工) (gōng, "work; labor") often refers to physical tasks or tasks that require effort and skill. 作(作) (zuò, "to make; to produce") indicates the action of producing or creating something. Together, 工作(工作) conveys the idea of engaging in labor or tasks to produce results or achieve goals.
etymology.interpretation: 工(工) (gōng, "work") conveys the idea of effort directed towards a task, often in a skilled trade or craft. 作(作) (zuò, "to make") adds the dynamic aspect of bringing something about through one's actions. Together, these elements form the concept of work as an ongoing activity where one's efforts produce tangible outputs or results.

parts.english: to make; to produce; to do; to act

examples.chinese: 他正在忙着工作(他正在忙著工作)。
她想找到一份理想的工作(她想找到一份理想的工作)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎng zhǎodào yī fèn lǐxiǎng de gōngzuò.
examples.english: She wants to find an ideal job.

examples.chinese: 工作结束后(工作結束後)，我们可以一起去喝咖啡(我們可以一起去喝咖啡)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngzuò jiéshù hòu, wǒmen kěyǐ yīqǐ qù hē kāfēi.
examples.english: After work ends, we can go for coffee together.
